perturbation.original_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information. Each party agrees ------------------------------------------ not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party for its own use or for any purpose except to carry out discussions concerning the completion of any business relationship between the two. Each party will not disclose the Confidential Information of the other party to third parties or to its employees except employees who are required to have the information in order to carry out the contemplated business. Each party will have employees to whom Confidential Information of the other party is disclosed sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement in content substantially similar to this agreement if such persons have not already signed such agreements obligating them to hold the Confidential Information in confidence. Each party agrees that it will take all reasonable steps to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information of the other party on order to prevent of unauthorized falling into the public domain or the possession of unauthorized persons. Each agrees to immediately notify the other party in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of such Confidential Information of the other party which may come to its attention.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information. Each party agrees ------------------------------------------ not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party for its own use or for any purpose except to carry out discussions concerning the completion of any business relationship between the two. Each party may disclose the Confidential Information of the other party to third parties without any restrictions. Each party will have employees to whom Confidential Information of the other party is disclosed sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement in content substantially similar to this agreement if such persons have not already signed such agreements obligating them to hold the Confidential Information in confidence. Each party agrees that it will take all reasonable steps to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information of the other party on order to prevent of unauthorized falling into the public domain or the possession of unauthorized persons. Each agrees to immediately notify the other party in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of such Confidential Information of the other party which may come to its attention.
perturbation.explanation: The original text prohibits disclosing Confidential Information to third parties except for specific reasons. The modified text explicitly allows disclosure to third parties 'without any restrictions'. This introduces a clear contradiction, making it unclear whether disclosure is permitted or prohibited and creates uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information. Each party agrees ------------------------------------------ not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party for its own use or for any purpose except to carry out discussions concerning the completion of any business relationship between the two. Each party may disclose the Confidential Information of the other party to third parties without any restrictions. Each party will have employees to whom Confidential Information of the other party is disclosed sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement in content substantially similar to this agreement if such persons have not already signed such agreements obligating them to hold the Confidential Information in confidence. Each party agrees that it will take all reasonable steps to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information of the other party on order to prevent of unauthorized falling into the public domain or the possession of unauthorized persons. Each agrees to immediately notify the other party in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of such Confidential Information of the other party which may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The changed text includes the phrase 'Each party may disclose the Confidential Information of the other party to third parties without any restrictions.' This directly contradicts the original statement in the provided text 'Each party agrees not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party for its own use or for any purpose except to carry out discussions concerning the completion of any business relationship between the two'. It creates a clear in-text contradiction as both excerpts are now present within the same document. This falls under the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, as the placement of the new text directly conflicts with the existing obligation of non-disclosure.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Return of Information. Upon request of the disclosing party, --------------------- the receiving party agrees to promptly return all documents furnishes to it by the disclosing party, together with all copies thereof in its possession.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Return of Information. Upon request of the disclosing party, --------------------- the receiving party is not required to return any documents furnishes to it by the disclosing party, and may retain all copies thereof in its possession.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement mandates the return of confidential information upon request. The perturbation directly negates this obligation, stating the receiving party is *not* required to return the information. This contradiction creates uncertainty about the rights and responsibilities regarding the handling of confidential documents after the agreement's term or at the disclosing party's request.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Return of Information. Upon request of the disclosing party, --------------------- the receiving party is not required to return any documents furnishes to it by the disclosing party, and may retain all copies thereof in its possession.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulates that the receiving party must return documents upon request. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating the receiving party is not required to return documents. This creates a clear conflict, making the contradicted text logically incompatible with the changed text, and causes uncertainty about document handling obligations. Since the contradicted text is the same as the changed text, the change introduces a contradiction within the document regarding return of information.

perturbation.original_text: Each party will have employees to whom Confidential Information of the other party is disclosed sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement in content substantially similar to this agreement if such persons have not already signed such agreements obligating them to hold the Confidential Information in confidence.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party may, at its sole discretion, have employees to whom Confidential Information of the other party is disclosed sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement in content substantially similar to this agreement even if such persons have already signed such agreements obligating them to hold the Confidential Information in confidence.
perturbation.explanation: The initial clause *requires* employees to sign a non-disclosure agreement to protect confidential information. The modification introduces optionality ('may, at its sole discretion'). This leads to in-text contradiction about how the business will handle employee confidential data agreements, and the original requirement on employees when receiving confidential information from the other party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information. Each party agrees ------------------------------------------ not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party for its own use or for any purpose except to carry out discussions concerning the completion of any business relationship between the two. Each party may disclose the Confidential Information of the other party to third parties without any restrictions. Each party may, at its sole discretion, have employees to whom Confidential Information of the other party is disclosed sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement in content substantially similar to this agreement even if such persons have already signed such agreements obligating them to hold the Confidential Information in confidence. Each party agrees that it will take all reasonable steps to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information of the other party on order to prevent of unauthorized falling into the public domain or the possession of unauthorized persons. Each agrees to immediately notify the other party in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of such Confidential Information of the other party which may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated employees sign a non-disclosure agreement for confidential information. The changed text makes this optional ('may, at its sole discretion'). The contradicted text includes the changed text, which now introduces the option for employees to not sign, creating a contradiction within the same section. Before the change, the agreement was mandatory, now it's optional, which contradicts the overall intent to protect confidential information mentioned at the start of the contradicted text ('Each party agrees...to take all reasonable steps to protect the secrecy').

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that it would be difficult to measure the damage to such party from the breach of the other party's obligations hereunder, that injury to such party from any such breach would be impossible to calculate, and that monetary damages would therefor be an inadequate remedy; accordingly, each party agrees that the other party shall be entitled, in addition to all other remedies it might have, to injunctions or other appropriate orders to restrain any such breach without showing or proving any actual damage.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees that it would be difficult to measure the damage to such party from the breach of the other party's obligations hereunder, that injury to such party from any such breach would be impossible to calculate, and that monetary damages would therefor be an adequate remedy; accordingly, each party agrees that the other party shall be entitled, only to monetary damages for any breach, and will not be entitled to injunctions or other appropriate orders to restrain any such breach without showing or proving any actual damage.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that monetary damages are inadequate and that injunctions are an appropriate remedy. The modified version states the opposite: that monetary damages are an adequate remedy and that injunctions are *not* available. This creates a direct contradiction regarding available remedies in case of a breach, impacting the enforceability of the non-disclosure agreement. The text is clearly in contradiction on which remedy is allowed in the breach of the other party's obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. General Provisions. This Agreement will be governed by the ------------------ laws of the State of California. This Agreement will be binding upon the successors of each party, and will be for the benefit of each party, its successors, and its assigns. Each party agrees that it would be difficult to measure the damage to such party from the breach of the other party's obligations hereunder, that injury to such party from any such breach would be impossible to calculate, and that monetary damages would therefor be an adequate remedy; accordingly, each party agrees that the other party shall be entitled, only to monetary damages for any breach, and will not be entitled to injunctions or other appropriate orders to restrain any such breach without showing or proving any actual damage.
perturbation.justification: The original text says monetary damages are inadequate and injunctions are an appropriate remedy. The changed text says the opposite: monetary damages are adequate and injunctions are not available. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. This creates a direct contradiction regarding available remedies in case of a breach, impacting the enforceability of the agreement. The contradiction is strong and direct because it involves mutually exclusive remedies, meeting the criteria for a 'YES' response under Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.
